message.content:
Search for TOP Design Studio "주소 변환" "Find & Replace" feature usage guide. I need to know:

1. How does "Find & Replace" work in TOP Design Studio address conversion tool?
2. Can you specify a source address range and target address range?
3. Does it support changing address TYPE (e.g., M → %MX, D → %MW)?
4. Can it convert between different PLC address formats (MASTER-K → XEC IEC)?
5. How to use "대상 주소" and "변경 주소" fields in Find & Replace mode?

- "TOP Design Studio" "주소 변환" "Find & Replace"

Based on the search results, I found information about TOP Design Studio and address conversion in PLC systems:

TOP Design Studio is a specialized software for designing and programming most of M2I's HMIs and supports working with various TOP-R, TOP-RX, and TOP-RH series HMI models.

Regarding the Find and Replace functionality with address conversion in PLC systems, the search results show examples from different platforms:

For address conversion in Pro-Face systems, you can change the type of device by specifying an address conversion pattern, and you need to designate the previous address range and the top address of the destination device/PLC.

For Studio 5000 (Rockwell Automation), you can use the Replace All option in the Find and Replace dialog box to replace all values for all of the properties that match search criteria simultaneously.

If you're looking for more specific information about the Find Replace address conversion feature in TOP Design Studio, you may want to consult the software's official documentation or user manual, as the search results provide limited detail about this particular feature in TOP Design Studio specifically.

content.content:
# PLC Type Conversion and Address Conversion Range

This procedure allows you to change a device/PLC type by specifying an address conversion pattern, designating the previous address range and the destination device's starting address.

## Key Steps:

1. Access **Project > System Settings > Device/PLC**
2. Select the target device tab and choose "Change Device/PLC"
3. Specify the new manufacturer and series in the dialog
4. Click "Change Specifying Address Conversion"
5. In the Address Conversion Method dialog, click "Add"
6. Configure conversion settings: Address Type, Before Conversion range (Start/End), and After Conversion start address
7. Execute the conversion

## Important Considerations:

After conversion, "any parts, D-Scripts, Alarms, etc., must have their device addresses set again." Additionally, screens using Special Switch settings for screen changes should be saved.

When converting multiple devices with Ethernet drivers, note that "UDP and TCP cannot be set up in the same driver"—meaning if one device uses UDP, another cannot simultaneously use TCP with the same driver type.

For detailed guidance, consult the Settings Guide referenced in the documentation.

I found relevant results for your search on "ADDRESS CONVERSION" PLC "source address" "target address" range. Here's what I found:

## Address Conversion in PLC Systems

The process involves accessing System Settings from the Project menu, selecting Device/PLC, clicking the Device/PLC2 tab, and clicking Change Device/PLC to set the Maker and Series of the device/PLC you want to change to.

To change the type of device, you specify an address conversion pattern and designate the previous address range and the top address of the destination device/PLC.

The process involves clicking "Change Specifying Address Conversion," then when the Address Conversion Method dialog appears, clicking "Add," and when the Register Address Conversion Pattern dialog appears, setting the Address Type, the Before Conversion addresses (Start and End), and the After Conversion address (Start).

After adding the conversion range to the Address Conversion Method dialog, you click Convert.

This information appears to be from Pro-Face documentation and relates to converting between different PLC types while maintaining proper address mapping between source and target systems.

I found search results related to TOP Design Studio and project menu conversion addresses. Based on the web search results, here's what I found:

In TOP Design Studio, you open a project file and go to the top menu [Project - Properties], then access the [Project Options] screen where you go to the [TOP Settings - Screen Conversion] page and check [Use Screen Conversion] and set the [Screen Number Write] address and [Screen Number Read] address.

The Screen Number Write address (HMI->PLC) is a function that writes the current screen number to a PLC address after the TOP converts screens. For example, if the value in PLC address D0 is 3, then the current operating screen in TOP is screen 3.

The Screen Number Read address (HMI<-PLC) reads the set PLC address value and converts to the screen number corresponding to that address value.

The main configuration involves accessing the Project Properties menu, navigating to the TOP Settings screen conversion page, and setting up the appropriate addresses for screen conversion operations.

content.text:
Based on my research, I need to provide you with an honest summary. Unfortunately, **detailed public documentation about TOP Design Studio's Find & Replace feature for address conversion is extremely limited** on the web. Here's what I found:

## Research Summary

### 1. **Available Information**
I found references to:
- **M2I Corporation's TOP Design Studio** (Korean HMI/SCADA software)
- A mention of **[Tools] - [Address/Object Search]** menu option in TOP Design Studio
- References to **address conversion** functionality in project settings
- **Pro-Face GP-Pro EX address conversion procedures** (similar concept for comparison)

### 2. **What I Could NOT Find**
Specific documentation about:
- How Find & Replace works in TOP Design Studio address conversion
- Whether it supports source/target address range specification
- Support for address TYPE changes (M → %MX, D → %MW)
- MASTER-K to XEC IEC format conversion capabilities
- "대상 주소" and "변경 주소" field usage details

### 3. **Why the Documentation Gap?**
- TOP Design Studio appears to be primarily marketed in Korean/Asian markets
- Official user manuals are very large PDFs (10-20MB) with compressed content that prevents easy web indexing
- This is specialized industrial software with limited English documentation online

## Recommended Next Steps

1. **Contact M2I Support**: Contact [M2I Corporation](https://www.m2i.co.kr) directly - they have a Korean technical support team
2. **Access Official Manual**: Download the full TOP Design Studio User Manual from:
   - [M2I Downloads Page](https://www.m2i.co.kr/service/download.php)
   - [Crevis Mirror (TDS_UserManual.pdf)](https://www.crevis.ru/files/M2I/TDS_UserManual.pdf)
3. **Check M2I FAQ**: [M2I FAQ](https://m2i.co.kr/service/faq.php) may have answers to your specific questions
4. **Reference Material**: [External Device Connection Manuals for TOP Design Studio](https://www.m2i.co.kr/uploaded/board/device/) - various device-specific manuals that demonstrate address mapping patterns

## Comparable Reference
For similar address conversion concepts, see [Pro-Face Address Conversion Documentation](https://www.pro-face.com/otasuke/files/manual/gpproex/new/refer/mergedProjects/connect/connect_pr_changeplc-addressrange.htm) which describes address type conversion patterns, source/target ranges, and PLC type conversion procedures that may apply to TOP Design Studio as well.

---

Sources:
- [M2I Corporation](https://www.m2i.co.kr)
- [TOP Design Studio User Manual](https://www.crevis.ru/files/M2I/TDS_UserManual.pdf)
- [Pro-Face Address Conversion Procedure](https://www.pro-face.com/otasuke/files/manual/gpproex/new/refer/mergedProjects/connect/connect_pr_changeplc-addressrange.htm)
- [PLC Memory Mapping and Addressing](https://instrumentationtools.com/plc-memory-mapping-io-addressing/)
